name: Anti-Dilution Protection
text: In the event the Company issues any new shares or other equity instruments at a price per share lower than the price per share paid by the existing Shareholders ('Down Round'), the affected Shareholders shall be entitled to anti-dilution protection in the form of a broad-based weighted average adjustment. The conversion price or subscription price applicable to such Shareholders shall be adjusted downward using the following formula: CP2 = CP1 × (A + B) ÷ (A + C), where CP1 is the existing conversion price, CP2 is the adjusted conversion price, A is the number of shares outstanding immediately prior to the new issuance, B is the number of shares that would have been issued at CP1 for the aggregate consideration received, and C is the number of shares actually issued. Any adjustment under this clause shall be subject to applicable provisions of the Companies Act, 2013, including requirements for shareholder approval for further issuance of shares.

legal_basis.note: Rights issue and new share issuance
Issue of shares at a discount — conditions

invalid_if: Adjustment formula is not clearly specified
Carve-outs for permitted issuances (ESOPs, bonus shares) are absent
title: Anti-Dilution Protection
dispute_hotspots: Down round triggering anti-dilution — calculation dispute
ESOP pool carve-out — whether ESOP issuance triggers anti-dilution
